MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues **Redacted**
Off Calendar Nov-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. TERRENCE J. COLEMAN & ATTY. RYAN H. OPGENORTH OF THE PILLSBURY/COLEMAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-433-8000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SEAN P. NALTY OF OGLETREE/DEAKINS, TEL. NO. 1-415-369-3552 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SECURITY MUTUAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY OF NEW YORK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION of Issues - The motion for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Defendant failed to maintain its initial summary adjudication burden of production. Defendant has not shown that it is "indisputable that the basis for the insurer's denial of benefits was reasonable." Wilson v. 21st Century Ins. Co. (2007) 42 Cal.4th 713, 724. The evidence establishes that defendant's doctors (Dr. DeFilippis and Dr. Nair) conceded that plaintiff was disabled from March 2013 through May 2014. (DeFilippis Depo., 79:3-19; 80:12-81:11; 107:23-108:2)(Nair Depo., 117:2-118:22). Defendant contends that plaintiff cannot meet the definition of partial disability because his loss of income was due to Mercury Insurance's decision to utilize in-house counsel. Defendant fails to establish that the use of in-house counsel rather than plaintiff's disability was the actual cause of his decrease in income, however. At the very least, there is a triable issue of material fact regarding that issue because plaintiff continued to receive work from Mercury Insurance after the transition. (McCormick Depo., 39:19 - 41:7). The court rejects defendant's argument that it cannot be liable because the third party administrator made the decision to deny coverage. That argument is raised for the first time in the reply and defendant can be liable based on principles of agency and ratification. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
